WebGPU_Local_LLM_Integration.axiomatic_intent: Enable local AI inference via WebGPU and WebLLM for high-performance, privacy-first model execution.

formal_proof.premise_1: Local models require high-performance execution environments.
formal_proof.premise_2: WebGPU provides near-native GPU access in the browser.
formal_proof.premise_3: WebLLM bridges local models and WebGPU.
formal_proof.premise_4: Integrating WebLLM with WebGPU satisfies the requirement for local AI inference.
formal_proof.conclusion: Therefore, implementing WebLLM over WebGPU enables the required local model execution.

steps.step_1: Verify WebGPU support in the browser environment.
steps.step_3: Implement fallback mechanisms for unsupported environments.

steps.step_1: Import and configure MLC WebLLM engine.
steps.step_2: Load Qwen 3.5 9B Quantized model weights into WebGPU memory.
steps.step_3: Establish message passing interface for chat and code completion.

steps.step_1: Implement secure storage for Gemini and other API keys.
steps.step_2: Create abstraction layer to switch between local WebLLM and remote APIs.
steps.step_3: Test end-to-end inference with both local and remote models.

Intuitive_GUI_and_Antigravity.axiomatic_intent: Develop a bleeding-edge, agent-first IDE interface with seamless chat, code editing, and novel 'antigravity' spatial interactions.

formal_proof.premise_1: An agent-first IDE requires seamless integration of chat and code editing.
formal_proof.premise_2: Bleeding-edge conceptual GUIs utilize spatial and physics-based interactions.
formal_proof.premise_3: Antigravity features provide a novel spatial interaction paradigm.
formal_proof.premise_4: Combining these elements creates the desired intuitive and advanced GUI.
formal_proof.conclusion: Therefore, implementing a spatial GUI with antigravity physics and integrated chat/code views fulfills the UI requirements.

steps.step_1: Design a flexible, node-based layout system for editor and chat windows.
steps.step_2: Implement drag-and-drop functionality with physics-based constraints.
steps.step_3: Integrate 'antigravity' physics using a 2D/3D physics engine (e.g., Matter.js or Three.js physics).

steps.step_1: Build a unified state manager for chat context and code editor state.
steps.step_2: Implement inline code querying and contextual chat overlays.
steps.step_3: Develop visual indicators linking chat messages to specific code blocks.

steps.step_1: Implement autonomous agent action visualizations (e.g., agent 'typing' or 'reading').
steps.step_2: Create UI for agent permission management and task approval.

Self_Improving_Agent_Architecture.user_data: Then, This Fruitbox will ultimately be a self-improving conversation, document editing, and code-editing platform that prioritizes powerful local agents like Qwen 3.5 9b Quantized.

Self_Improving_Agent_Architecture.axiomatic_intent: Establish a self-improving architecture where local agents iteratively refine conversations, documents, and code.

formal_proof.premise_1: Self-improvement requires a feedback loop of action, evaluation, and refinement.
formal_proof.premise_2: Local agents (Qwen 3.5 9B) can perform actions and evaluate outcomes.
formal_proof.premise_3: A structured memory and versioning system tracks refinements.
formal_proof.premise_4: Connecting the agent's evaluation to the memory system enables iterative refinement.
formal_proof.conclusion: Therefore, an architecture combining local agents with a feedback-driven memory system achieves self-improvement.

steps.step_1: Implement a system for capturing user corrections and implicit feedback.
steps.step_2: Create an evaluation module for the agent to assess its own code/document edits.
steps.step_3: Develop a version control system for tracking iterative improvements.

steps.step_1: Design a task router that prioritizes local models (Qwen 3.5 9B) for specific tasks.
steps.step_2: Implement fallback logic to cloud APIs when local models lack capability or context.
steps.step_3: Optimize local model inference for continuous background tasks.

steps.step_1: Develop a mechanism for the agent to update its system prompt based on past interactions.
steps.step_2: Implement a local fine-tuning or RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline for long-term memory.
steps.step_3: Test self-improvement cycles on sample codebases.

Neurosymbolic_Vector_Persona.axiomatic_intent: Create an interactive avatar persona driven by a hybrid neurosymbolic and LLM architecture with vector-state memory.

formal_proof.premise_1: A 'soulful' persona requires consistent memory, logical reasoning, and natural language generation.
formal_proof.premise_2: Vector-state memory provides consistent long-term context.
formal_proof.premise_3: Neurosymbolic models provide logical reasoning and rule-based behavior.
formal_proof.premise_4: Hybrid LLMs provide natural language generation.
formal_proof.conclusion: Therefore, combining vector-state memory, a neurosymbolic model, and a hybrid LLM creates the required interactive persona.

steps.step_1: Implement a local vector database (e.g., using IndexedDB or a WASM-based vector store).
steps.step_2: Develop embedding generation for user interactions and persona states.
steps.step_3: Create retrieval mechanisms for contextualizing the persona's responses.

steps.step_1: Design a symbolic rule engine for core persona traits and logical constraints.
steps.step_2: Integrate the rule engine with the LLM prompt generation pipeline.
steps.step_3: Implement a feedback loop where LLM outputs are validated against symbolic rules.

steps.step_1: Develop a 3D or 2D animated avatar interface.
steps.step_2: Map neurosymbolic states (emotions, logic states) to avatar animations.
steps.step_3: Integrate voice synthesis and recognition for multimodal interaction.

CodeMirror6_Agentic_Editor.axiomatic_intent: To engineer a hyper-reactive, context-aware CodeMirror 6 interface that seamlessly fuses manual human coding with autonomous, inline AI agent actions and neurosymbolic validation.

formal_proof.premise_1: An industry-leading agentic IDE requires an editor that understands code semantics and agent intents.
formal_proof.premise_2: CodeMirror 6 provides a highly extensible, state-driven architecture suitable for complex UI overlays and LSP integrations.
formal_proof.premise_3: Integrating local LLMs (WebLLM) directly into the editor state enables zero-latency ghost text and inline refactoring.
formal_proof.premise_4: Combining CM6 extensibility with local AI creates a seamless human-agent collaborative surface.
formal_proof.conclusion: Therefore, extending CodeMirror 6 with LSP, inline AI widgets, and spatial block manipulation fulfills the requirement for an industry-leading agentic editor.

steps.step_1: Integrate Language Server Protocol (LSP) via codemirror-languageserver for rich intellisense.
steps.step_2: Implement advanced code folding, minimap, and multi-language dynamic loading.
steps.step_3: Establish real-time syntax and linting diagnostic pipelines.

steps.step_1: Develop inline ghost-text completion driven by local Qwen 3.5 9B model.
steps.step_2: Create inline diff widgets for agent-proposed refactoring and code generation.
steps.step_3: Implement semantic code selection (vector-based querying of highlighted blocks).

steps.step_1: Enable 'antigravity' code blocks (detachable, floating editor fragments).
steps.step_2: Integrate neurosymbolic logic validation with real-time visual feedback in the gutter.
steps.step_3: Implement multi-cursor support for simultaneous human and agent editing.
